questions.question: Which property type is best suited for indicating whether a condition is true or false?

options.text: Select
options.explanation: Select allows you to choose one option from a list of tags, not necessarily boolean states.

options.text: Checkbox
options.explanation: The documentation states that a 'Checkbox' is used to indicate whether a condition is true or false.

options.text: Status
options.explanation: Status tracks progress using tags like 'To-do' or 'Complete', which is more complex than a simple true/false check.

options.text: Number
options.explanation: Number accepts numerical values, which are not boolean states.

questions.question: How do you add a property to a database that already has existing properties?

options.text: Click 'Add property' at the bottom of the database.
options.explanation: This method is for creating your first property, not adding to an existing one.

options.text: Select the '+' next to the right-most property and give it a name.
options.explanation: The article specifies selecting the '+' next to the right-most property to add a new one.

options.text: Go to the Edit properties menu and click 'New property'.
options.explanation: While this creates a new property, the specific instruction for adding to an existing list in a table is the '+' button.

options.text: Double-click any existing cell to insert a new column.
options.explanation: Double-clicking a cell edits the value, not the property definition.

questions.question: What feature allows you to set a reminder for a Date property?

options.text: Date format & timezone
options.explanation: This is used to modify the display settings of the date, not set a reminder.

options.text: Remind
options.explanation: The documentation notes that clicking 'Remind' will notify you on the given date and time.

options.text: Include time
options.explanation: This option chooses an exact time for the date picker, but 'Remind' is the specific button for notifications.

options.text: Clear
options.explanation: Clear removes any value from the property.

questions.question: How do you hide a specific property from a database view?

options.text: Click the eye icon next to the property in the Edit properties menu.
options.explanation: The visibility menu is accessed from the database view settings, not the Edit properties menu.

options.text: Select the slider icon at the top of the database view, then Property visibility, and toggle the eye icon.
options.explanation: The steps clearly state: Open settings menu, select Property visibility, and select the eye icon to hide/show.

options.text: Delete the property from the database settings.
options.explanation: Deleting removes the property entirely; hiding just makes it invisible in the current view.

options.text: Right-click the property header and choose 'Hide'.
options.explanation: There is no 'Hide' option in the right-click menu for property headers.

questions.question: Which properties are automatically recorded by Notion and cannot be edited by the user?

options.text: Text and Select
options.explanation: Text and Select are manually editable properties.

options.text: Created time, Created by, Last edited time, Last edited by
options.explanation: These four properties are listed as auto-generated and not editable.

options.text: Number and Date
options.explanation: Number and Date are manually entered by the user.

options.text: URL and Email
options.explanation: URL and Email are manually entered by the user.

questions.question: In which view type can you apply conditional color to a specific property without coloring the entire page background?

options.text: Board view
options.explanation: The Board view is not mentioned as supporting this distinction.

options.text: Timeline view
options.explanation: The Timeline view is not mentioned as supporting this distinction.

options.text: Table view
options.explanation: The article states: 'To apply conditional color to a property and not an entire database page, you must be in a table view of a database.'

options.text: Calendar view
options.explanation: The Calendar view applies colors to the page level background.

questions.question: Which of the following properties does NOT allow commenting directly in table view?

options.text: Formula
options.explanation: The documentation explicitly lists Formula as a property type where you cannot comment.

options.text: Rollup
options.explanation: Rollup is explicitly listed as a property type where you cannot comment.

options.text: Text
options.explanation: You can comment in Text properties in table view.

options.text: Button
options.explanation: Button is explicitly listed as a property type where you cannot comment.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Rollup property?

options.text: To connect two different databases together.
options.explanation: This describes the Relation property.

options.text: To perform calculations based on other properties.
options.explanation: This describes the Formula property.

options.text: To view and aggregate information about properties from relation properties.
options.explanation: The documentation defines Rollup as 'View and aggregate information about properties from relation properties'.

options.text: To store files and images.
options.explanation: This describes the File property.

questions.question: How do you delete a tag in a Select or Multi-Select property?

options.text: Right-click the tag and select Delete.
options.explanation: There is no right-click option mentioned for deleting tags.

options.text: Click the property field (i.e., the table cell), then click the ••• that appears to the right.
options.explanation: The documentation instructs to click the property field to reveal the ••• menu where tags can be edited or deleted.

options.text: Click the tag and press Backspace.
options.explanation: Pressing Backspace usually deletes the value in the cell, not the tag option itself.

options.text: Select the tag and drag it to the trash can icon.
options.explanation: Dragging is used to reorder tags, not delete them.

questions.question: Which view is NOT supported for applying conditional color?

options.text: Feed
options.explanation: Feed is listed as a supported view.

options.text: Board
options.explanation: Board is listed as a supported view.

options.text: Gallery
options.explanation: The article lists supported views as Table, Calendar, Timeline, List, Board, and Feed. Gallery is not included.

options.text: List
options.explanation: List is listed as a supported view.

questions.question: How do you remove a person tag from a Person property?

options.text: Click the person's name and press Enter.
options.explanation: Pressing Enter adds the person.

options.text: Click the person, then click the X next to their name.
options.explanation: The instructions state to click the person and then click the X next to their name to delete them.

options.text: Click the ••• menu next to the property.
options.explanation: The ••• menu is for property settings, not individual person tags.

options.text: Drag the tag to the edge of the property field.
options.explanation: Dragging is not a valid method to remove a person tag.

questions.question: Which setting allows you to define a range of dates in a Date property?

options.text: Include time
options.explanation: Include time is used to select an exact moment, not a range.

options.text: End date
options.explanation: Switching on 'End date' allows you to define a range of dates in the property.

options.text: Date format
options.explanation: Date format changes how the date is displayed.

options.text: Reminder
options.explanation: Reminder sets a notification for a specific date.

questions.question: If you only have 'View' or 'Comment' access to a database, what can you do regarding conditional colors?

options.text: You can see the colors but cannot edit them.
options.explanation: The note states: 'If you can only view or comment on a database, you can see color for database pages but can’t edit any color settings.'

options.text: You can edit colors but cannot see them.
options.explanation: This contradicts the documentation; you cannot see the colors if you lack edit permissions.

options.text: You cannot see the colors at all.
options.explanation: You can see the colors, but not edit them.

options.text: You can edit colors as long as you don't change the background color.
options.explanation: You cannot edit any color settings.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid way to upload a file to a Files & media property?

options.text: Click the field and paste a link.
options.explanation: Pasting a link to embed a file is a valid method.

options.text: Drag a file from your computer into the property.
options.explanation: Dragging a file is a valid method of uploading.

options.text: Type the file name into the property.
options.explanation: You must click the field to prompt an upload or paste a link; simply typing the name does not upload the file.

options.text: Select the field and click 'Upload file'.
options.explanation: This is a valid method implied by the UI description.
